ingredients: 1 cup of fine corn flour (for the pap)
cooking_steps: Mix flour, yeast, sugar, and nutmeg in a bowl. Gradually add warm water while stirring until you get a thick, stretchy batter.
Cover the bowl with a cloth and let the batter rise for 45 minutes to 1 hour until it doubles in size and becomes bubbly.
Heat vegetable oil in a deep pan. Scoop small handfuls of the batter and drop them into the hot oil, frying until golden brown on all sides.
Separately, mix the corn flour with a little cold water to form a smooth paste, then stir it into boiling water until it thickens into a smooth porridge (pap).
Serve the warm puff-puff alongside a bowl of pap for a classic energizing breakfast.

meal_name: Sweet Potato, Rice, Greens and Meat
ingredients: 1 cup of white or jasmine rice
2 cups of njama-njama (huckleberry leaves), washed and chopped
200g of lean beef, cut into small pieces
cooking_steps: Peel and boil the sweet potatoes until soft, then set aside.
Cook the rice separately until fluffy and set aside.
In a pan, heat oil and sauté the sliced onion until soft. Add the beef and cook until browned and tender.
Add the chopped njama-njama to the pan and stir for 5 minutes until wilted, seasoning to taste.
Plate the boiled sweet potato and rice together, then top with the greens and beef mixture.
Serve warm as a balanced midday meal to sustain energy through the afternoon.

ingredients: 1 whole fresh tilapia, cleaned and scaled
3 pieces of miondo (fermented cassava wrapped in leaves)
cooking_steps: Rinse the tilapia and pat dry. Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice, then let it marinate for 15 minutes.
Grill the fish over medium heat for 6 to 7 minutes on each side until the skin is crispy and the flesh is fully cooked.
Unwrap the miondo from its leaf wrapping and slice into portions.
Arrange the grilled fish beside the miondo, garnished with sliced onion and a squeeze of fresh lemon.
Serve as a light, protein-rich evening meal before rest.

meal_name: Kumba Bread with Akara, Pap and Pepper
ingredients: 2 rounds of kumba bread (or soft local bread rolls)
1 cup of fine corn flour (for the pap)
cooking_steps: Soak the black-eyed peas and remove the skins, then blend with the onion and a small amount of water until smooth and fluffy.
Season the bean batter with salt, then scoop spoonfuls into hot oil, frying until golden brown to make the akara (bean cakes).
Prepare the pap by stirring corn flour into boiling water until it forms a smooth, thick porridge.
Split the kumba bread open and tuck two or three akara pieces inside, adding a touch of blended pepper for heat.
Serve the stuffed bread alongside the warm pap for a hearty, protein-packed start to the day.

ingredients: 1 piece of roasted mackerel fish or lean boiled goat meat
A small handful of roasted groundnuts
cooking_steps: Cook the waterfufu paste in boiling water, stirring vigorously until it forms a smooth, heavy, solid ball.
Steam the shredded eru leaves gently with a small amount of water alongside the mackerel fish or goat meat.
Mix the ingredients smoothly to ensure the natural protein juices flavor the greens.
Serve the hot waterfufu next to the rich, protein-dense eru stew.
Eat within 2 hours of a heavy lifting session to maximize muscle tissue rebuilding.

ingredients: 400g of lean beef, cut into strips
cooking_steps: Marinate the beef strips in suya spice mix for at least 15 minutes.
Skewer the beef and grill over hot coals or a grill pan for 3 to 4 minutes per side until browned but still juicy.
Peel and grill the plantain slices until golden on both sides.
Arrange the suya skewers alongside the grilled plantain, garnished with sliced onion and crushed groundnuts.
Serve warm within an hour of training for maximum protein uptake and muscle recovery.
Soak the black-eyed peas and remove the skins, then blend into a smooth, thick paste with a small amount of water.
Mix in the red palm oil, ground crayfish, blended pepper, and salt until well combined.
Wrap portions of the mixture in banana leaves, folding securely into small parcels.
Steam the wrapped parcels for 45 minutes to 1 hour until firm and fully cooked.
Unwrap and serve warm as a fiber-rich, plant-protein breakfast that supports steady insulin response.

ingredients: 3 clean slices of white or yellow yam
2 cups of chopped fresh pumpkin leaves
1 half-teaspoon of unrefined raw palm oil
cooking_steps: Boil the yam slices until a fork passes through easily, then drain.
Mix the ground egusi with water to form a thick paste.
Boil the egusi paste gently with the chopped pumpkin leaves and a tiny drop of raw palm oil to create a thick vegetable purée.
Boil the lemongrass in hot water separately for 5 minutes to create a metabolic-clearing tea.
Serve the boiled yam alongside the egusi greens with the lemongrass tea to help balance insulin sensitivity.

meal_name: Light Pepper Soup with Garden Egg
ingredients: 200g of light fish or chicken pieces
1 tablespoon of pepper soup spice mix
cooking_steps: Bring water to a boil and add the ginger, garlic, and pepper soup spice mix.
Add the fish or chicken pieces and simmer for 15 to 20 minutes until fully cooked.
Add the sliced garden eggs and cook for a further 5 minutes until just tender.
Season with salt to taste, keeping the broth light and clear.
Serve hot as a warming, low-carb evening meal that supports overnight digestion and recovery.
